title: $RTL_APPLICATION
text: $RTL_APPLICATION Specify that the desktop application will read from right to left instead of from left to right. This causes the layout of all forms and their contents to be mirrored, so that the starting point for reading is the right side. $RTL_APPLICATION Defaults Assignment file: Any application assignment file Section: [SETTINGS] Default value: None Description By setting $RTL_APPLICATION in the application assignment file, the application components and their contents are displayed as a mirror image of the appearance in the Uniface IDE. The setting is applied to the application, even when running on a left-to-right system. Related Links $RTL_FIELDS Set the position of the cursor in the field at deployment. For right-to-left applications only. $RTL_PRINTING Determine processing required when printing right-to-left fields. RtlAttachMirroring This initialization setting controls how attachment works for widgets, entities, and area frames when running in a right-to-left (RTL) application or form. Attach to Window Border (attach) The Attach to Window Border (Attach) widget property attaches the widget (or area frame) to one or more form window borders or split bars at runtime. This allows the element to be resized or moved when the window is resized or a split bar moved. Direction Window property of a form that specifies the reading orientation of the form at runtime. If set to right-to-left, the layout and text orientation are mirrored. Desktop Applications for Bidirectional Languages Uniface supports bidirectional languages such as Arabic and Hebrew, which are read from right-to-left (RTL), and enables you to design and create applications for such languages. Although you need to develop the application on a left-to-right (LTR) system, you can run, test, and deploy on both LTR and RTL systems. Types of Character Sets The installable character sets can be either single-byte or double-byte. Single-byte character sets can be divided into left-to-right and right-to-left.
